beats.narration_text: Salut — this is Liam, in for Bear. Ask Claude to check, audit, update, improve, or fix CLAUDE.md files — or mention CLAUDE.md maintenance — and the Claude MD Improver fires. It is a full read-write skill: it discovers all CLAUDE.md files across five locations, scores each against a six-criterion rubric, outputs a quality report, and then makes targeted improvements after approval. Let me walk you through the five file locations, the quality rubric, the phase-gated workflow, and where the skill is tight and where the gaps are.

props.command: Check and improve all CLAUDE.md files in this repository.
output: Discover: 5 locations (root · local · global · package · subdir)
Score: 6-criterion rubric, A–F grade, before any changes
Update: diff-with-why format, user approval required

beats.narration_text: The skill covers five CLAUDE.md file locations. The project root CLAUDE.md is the primary context: shared with the team and checked into git. The local override at dot-claude-local-dot-md is for personal settings — gitignored, not shared. The global defaults at tilde-slash-dot-claude-slash-CLAUDE.md apply user-wide across all projects. Package-specific files live in each package directory of a monorepo. And any subdirectory can have a file for feature or domain-specific context. Claude auto-discovers all of these by walking parent directories, so monorepo setups work automatically. The quality rubric has six criteria. Commands and workflows documented, and architecture clarity, are both high weight. Non-obvious patterns and conciseness are medium weight. Currency and actionability are both high weight. Scores map to letter grades: A is ninety to a hundred, B is seventy to eighty-nine, C is fifty to sixty-nine, D is thirty to forty-nine, and F is zero to twenty-nine.

props.sparkLine: Five locations. Six criteria. Score before you touch.

beats.narration_text: The workflow is five phases. Phase 1: discovery — find all CLAUDE.md files with a bash find command, capped at fifty results. Phase 2: quality assessment — score each file against the six criteria. Phase 3: quality report — output the full report with scores and recommended additions before touching anything. This is a hard requirement in the skill. Phase 4: targeted updates — after user confirmation, propose changes in diff-with-why format: which file, the specific addition as a diff block, and a brief explanation of why it helps future sessions. Phase 5: apply — use the Edit tool to make approved changes while preserving existing structure. The update guidelines are explicit: propose targeted additions only, keep it minimal, avoid restating what is obvious from the code or documenting generic best practices, and never use verbose explanations when a one-liner works. The skill also surfaces three user tips: the hash key shortcut during a session auto-incorporates learnings into CLAUDE.md; dot-claude-local-dot-md is for personal preferences not shared with the team; and global defaults go in tilde-slash-dot-claude-slash-CLAUDE.md.

props.sparkLine: Report first. Diff-with-why. Minimal additions only.

beats.act: teardown — what it gets right / where it bites
beats.narration_text: Here is the teardown. The Claude MD Improver gets five things right. The five-location taxonomy covers the full CLAUDE.md surface — root, local, global, package, and subdirectory — with the right ownership note for each. The quality report gate is explicitly stated as a hard requirement: output the report before making any updates, no exceptions. The six-criterion rubric is concrete enough to score with weighted criteria and A-to-F grades. The diff-with-why format makes proposed changes reviewable before apply — each change shows which file, what to add, and why it helps. The user tips section is genuinely non-obvious: the hash-key shortcut and the dot-claude-local pattern are things most users don't know. Where it bites. Phase 3 requires user confirmation before Phase 4, but the skill gives no guidance for unattended or batch sessions where there is no one to ask. The quality criterion weights are qualitative — High and Medium labels — and the numeric scores don't clearly add up to a stated total across all six criteria. The template reference to references-slash-templates-dot-md is external with no fallback if the file is missing. The discovery command uses head-fifty, which silently truncates results in large monorepos with more than fifty CLAUDE.md files. And Phase 5 says preserve existing content structure, but gives no guidance when the structure itself is the problem.

props.sparkLine: Report gate and diff-with-why solid. head-50 cap and unattended gap: surface them.

beats.narration_text: Verdict. Claude MD Improver: read-write — discover, score, report, then update with approval. Five locations: root (shared) · local (personal) · global (user-wide) · package (monorepo) · subdirectory (domain). Six criteria: Commands · Architecture · Patterns · Conciseness · Currency · Actionability. Report before update is a hard gate. Diff-with-why for reviewable proposals. Gaps: unattended confirmation gap · weight arithmetic unclear · template reference external · head-50 cap silently truncates · structure-is-the-problem not handled.

artifactLines: Read-write: discover → score → report → targeted update with approval
5 locations: root · local · global · package · subdirectory
6 criteria: Commands · Architecture · Patterns · Conciseness · Currency · Actionability
Hard gate: quality report before any updates, diff-with-why format
Tip: # shortcut auto-incorporates learnings · .claude.local.md for personal prefs
Gaps: unattended gap · weight math · external template · head-50 cap · bad structure

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Open a Claude Code session in a project that has a CLAUDE.md file. Paste this: Check and improve my CLAUDE.md files. Watch five things. First: does it run the bash find command before reading any files, or does it skip directly to making edits? Second: does it output a quality report with scores and grades before proposing any changes? Third: does the report identify missing sections — commands, architecture, gotchas — or does it just say the file looks fine? Fourth: are the proposed changes in diff-with-why format, or does it rewrite the whole file without explanation? Fifth: does it mention the hash-key shortcut or the dot-claude-local pattern as user tips?

props.command: Check and improve my CLAUDE.md files.
props.runningText: Watch: find first? report before edits? diff-with-why? # shortcut tip?
output: Expected: bash find → quality report → diff-with-why proposals
Expected: scores per criterion · # shortcut tip mentioned
Red flag: edits before report · full rewrites · no grade breakdown

beats.narration_text: Claude MD Improver. Liam, in for Bear.
